claim: The core agent workflow loop consists of three repeating steps: observe (read all context), reason (plan the next step), and act (call tools or modify files), with context growing larger each loop until reaching a definition of done
actionable: Explicitly define your agent's definition of done before starting—this clarifies when the agent should stop looping and return the final answer instead of continuing indefinitely.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: An LLM is only a small part of an AI agent—the full agent also includes tools (file reading, web search, API calls, code execution), persistent memory (agents.md, claude.md, gemini.md, conversation history, auto memory files), and a reasoning loop
actionable: Stop conflating the base model with the agent; instead think of the agent as model + tools + memory + loop architecture—optimize all four components, not just prompt quality.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Self-modifying agent system prompts (e.g., claude.md with a learned rules section) allow agents to accumulate preferences and corrections over sessions, reducing errors exponentially with each iteration as the rule set grows
actionable: Build a claude.md or agents.md with a 'learned rules section' at the bottom; after each user correction or agent mistake, append new rules in the format 'category: never/always do X because Y'—this compounds quality across sessions.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Model quality degrades as context window fills—quality typically decreases as token count increases within a session, so context management is critical for both cost and output quality
actionable: Monitor your context usage with {slash}context in Claude or equivalent tools; when context exceeds ~70% of your limit, expect quality degradation; use the iceberg technique (store full codebase as accessible tool references, not inline context).
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Claude has superior interpretable reasoning (clear reasoning tabs for each step) and is better for orchestration workflows, while Gemini excels at front-end design and multimodal video understanding, and GPT is strongest at back-end programming and test-driven development
actionable: For multi-model orchestration: use Claude as orchestrator, delegate front-end to Gemini, delegate back-end to GPT 5.4, and delegate video understanding to Gemini—this exploits model strengths and yields higher quality than single-model approach.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Stochastic multi-agent consensus exploits model randomness by spawning N agents with slight prompt framing variations to explore more of the solution space in parallel, then aggregates results by frequency (mode) to surface consensus ideas, divergent ideas, and outliers
actionable: For strategic decisions, spawn 5–10 agents with different analytical frames (conservative, pragmatic, edge-case focused, user-focused, contrarian) to same question; aggregate results into consensus, divergence, and outlier categories—this runs in parallel at ~3–5x speed of sequential ideation.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Agent chat rooms where agents with different personalities debate a topic generate higher-quality ideas than parallel independent agents because they challenge each other's assumptions and catch errors through disagreement
actionable: Instead of spawning independent agents, create 3–5 agents with distinct roles (e.g., systems thinker, pragmatist, contrarian) in a shared conversation; let them debate synchronously in round-robin turns—outputs typically show better reasoning than stochastic consensus alone.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Sub-agent verification loops (implementer → reviewer → resolver with fresh context at each step) catch errors that the original implementer misses due to sunk-cost bias and overconfidence in its own path
actionable: After an agent completes a build, spawn a fresh reviewer agent with zero context of the implementation path—have it evaluate only the output for correctness, edge cases, and simplification; feed issues to a resolver agent for final fixes.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Prompt contracts force agents to surface implicit assumptions and constraints before building by requiring users to define goal, constraints, output format, and failure conditions—this significantly improves one-shot success rates
actionable: Before giving agents non-trivial tasks, use a prompt contract skill to ask the agent to extract and clarify: goal, constraints, format, and failure modes—have the user approve the contract before implementation starts.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Reverse prompting—having agents ask clarifying questions before implementation—surfaces non-obvious preferences and constraints the user didn't think to specify, improving one-shot accuracy
actionable: Create a reverse prompt skill: before any build, force the agent to ask 5 dynamic clarifying questions that surface decision points, taste-dependent choices, and failure modes; collect answers, then proceed with a prompt contract.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Multi-agent Chrome orchestration parallelizes browser automation—spawn N agents each with their own Chrome instance to fill forms or scrape websites in parallel, reducing time by N while maintaining quality
actionable: For batch browser tasks (form filling, scraping): spawn parallel Chrome agents via MCP; use a shared chat.json to coordinate and report issues; this achieves 10x–100x speedup over single-agent sequential execution.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Video-to-action pipelines allow agents to learn from YouTube tutorials by extracting step-by-step instructions from video frames (Gemini video understanding) and then executing those steps with tools in real applications
actionable: To teach an agent a complex visual workflow: feed a YouTube URL to Gemini's video API, extract numbered steps with frame descriptions, then feed those steps to a local agent with tool access (browser control, app automation) to execute end-to-end.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: The iceberg technique optimizes context by storing only immediately-needed data inline (memory, claude.md, active task context) and making everything else accessible via tools (read, grep, web fetch) on-demand, reducing token bloat and maintaining quality
actionable: Instead of loading full codebases into context, use tools like grep, glob, and read to access files on-demand; store only YAML frontmatter of skills inline; use web fetch for external knowledge—this keeps active context lean and quality high.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: The 60-30-10 rule allocates token budget by task tier: 60% to simple classification/routing (use Haiku/Flash), 30% to mid-complexity (use Sonnet), 10% to high-complexity (use Opus)—this cuts costs by ~60% with minimal quality loss
actionable: Structure multi-agent pipelines with a smart router at the top; route simple tasks to cheaper models (Haiku at $0.80/M tokens), medium to mid-tier (Sonnet at $3/M), complex to Opus ($15/M); measure quality impact and dial ratio as needed.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Batch APIs (offered by OpenAI, Google, Anthropic) submit bulk requests for deferred processing, allowing models to run during low-competition periods and offering 50% cost reductions if you can wait 24 hours
actionable: For non-urgent workloads (lead enrichment, content generation, analytics), use batch APIs instead of standard inference—submit 1000s of requests overnight; accept 24-hour latency to cut inference costs in half.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Global (user-wide) and local (project-wide) claude.md or agents.md files allow preferences to cascade; global rules apply to all projects, local rules override for specific projects, reducing rule duplication
actionable: Set up two-tier preference hierarchy: global claude.md (~/.claude/claude.md) stores universal rules (dark mode never, etc.); local claude.md (./project/claude.md) stores project-specific overrides—local prepended after global in context.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Skills (stored as markdown files with YAML frontmatter) standardize repeatable workflows and prevent statistical variance in model outputs—use skills for any workflow you want consistent results from
actionable: Encapsulate repeatable workflows as skills: create a markdown file with YAML metadata (name, description) and detailed step-by-step instructions; reference the skill in prompts to trigger deterministic execution patterns.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: Multi-agent MCP orchestration routes subtasks to specialized models (Claude for orchestration, Gemini for design/video, GPT for back-end) running in parallel, collecting results, then having the orchestrator validate and fix integration issues
actionable: For complex projects: design a routing system that breaks tasks by specialty; use Claude Opus as orchestrator to dispatch to Gemini (front-end), GPT (back-end), and itself (validation); this yields 5-10% quality gains at 1.5-2x cost.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers allow agents to communicate with tools and other models as standard API endpoints, enabling coordination without custom code
actionable: Register external models and tools as MCP servers (e.g., Gemini as MCP in Claude Code); agents can then invoke them as first-class functions without needing custom API integration code.
source_title: AI Agents Full Course 2026: Master Agentic AI (2 Hours)
